question_text: Use the graph shown below. The line represents the equation 2x + y = 4. At which value of x does the line cross the x-axis?

answer_key.explanation: Setting y = 0 in 2x + y = 4 gives x = 2, so the x-intercept is at (2, 0).
common_mistakes: Reading the y-intercept instead of the x-intercept.
answer_key.related_concept: Graph of a linear equation in two variables

visual.caption: Straight line on the coordinate plane.
visual.altText: A straight line drawn from upper left to lower right on a coordinate grid, crossing both axes.
